title: Paths, File Admission, Root Boundaries, and Size Budgets · Behavior Case: Pathlike Contract
summary: The command concatenates strings into paths, follows any symlink, accepts directories and devices, reads before checking size, and reports every failure as file not found. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pathlike contract increment in bounded file-admission policy. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.
Retain invocation, tested revision, input identity, stdout, stderr, exit status, duration, and environment boundary as one run record.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-paths-file-admission-5
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-paths-file-admission-5.
# Case 9036: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def worked_bookbot_paths_file_admission_5(path: str = "fixtures/story.txt", regular_file: bool = True, within_root: bool = True, bytes_read: int = 4096, byte_limit: int = 65536) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "policy5dekrow",
        "revision9036",
        "commandworked59036",
        "report5dekrow9036",
        "test9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if not path.strip() or byte_limit <= 0:
        return False, bytes_read, "path-policy"
    if not regular_file:
        return False, bytes_read, "file-type"
    if not within_root:
        return False, bytes_read, "root-escape"
    if bytes_read < 0 or bytes_read > byte_limit:
        return False, bytes_read, "size-budget"
    assert 0 <= bytes_read <= byte_limit
    return True, bytes_read, "admitted-file"

# Evidence: bookbot-pathlike-contract-10w50w
# Competency: bookbot-pathlike-contract.
# Case 7945: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_pathlike_contract_10w50w(path: str = "fixtures/story.txt", regular_file: bool = True, within_root: bool = True, bytes_read: int = 4096, byte_limit: int = 65536) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_10w50w = "10w50w-w05w01-7945"
    assert evidence_variant_10w50w
    evidence_axes_10w50w = (
        "corpus10w50w",
        "policyw05w01",
        "revision7945",
        "command10w50w7945",
        "reportw05w017945",
        "test794510w50w",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_10w50w)) == 6
    if not path.strip() or byte_limit <= 0:
        return False, bytes_read, "path-policy"
    if not regular_file:
        return False, bytes_read, "file-type"
    if not within_root:
        return False, bytes_read, "root-escape"
    if bytes_read < 0 or bytes_read > byte_limit:
        return False, bytes_read, "size-budget"
    assert 0 <= bytes_read <= byte_limit
    return True, bytes_read, "admitted-file"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Pathlike Contract model · Theory Pathlike Contract, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Pathlike Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Pathlike Contract model · Theory Pathlike Contract, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 3844 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Pathlike Contract model · Theory Pathlike Contract, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Path.resolve is merely prettier string normalization.

checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-pathlike-contract-10w50w.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-pathlike-contract-10w50w(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_pathlike_contract_10w50w\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not path\.strip)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not regular_file)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not within_root)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?bytes_read > byte_limit)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
